governance.registry_check: PASS — no frozen registry object (registry/verdicts, registry/experiments, registry/assumptions, registry/assessments) created or modified; all outputs confined to poc/p3-power/. This run writes NO verdict and NO KB shard, per POWER.md §0 and the rig's ILLUSTRATIVE-ONLY gate.

headline.question: Can perfect covered-item correction reach the win-margin delta_k on a GENERAL index, from the EXISTING coverage censuses?
headline.answer: NO free general-index kill is computable from the existing censuses — but NOT for the reason the steering premise assumed. The blocker is census INCOMPLETENESS, not nonzero coverage. Where coverage IS measured it is exactly zero (0/1550 define-lane; g8 0/1000 Mathlib), which drives a domain-scoped (D3) ceiling to 0. A whole-index kill is arithmetically impossible until the whole frozen suite is censused, because every uncensused component rides at FULL HEADROOM (union-bound completeness rule, POWER.md §3.1).
headline.verdict: ILLUSTRATIVE-ONLY — no G1 verdict (provisional pin frozen=false; no census pin block; no registered Form-A eligibility).

runs.label: RUN-1 — MEASURED define-lane census (the load-bearing run)
runs.tag: MEASURED coverage (0/1550), MEASURED delta_max upper bound; PROVISIONAL index pin
runs.measured_coverage: 7 D3 benchmarks, covered_count = 0 on every one; N_total = 500+144+100+100+135+265+306 = 1550; kappa_hat = 0.0000 exhaustively

decomposition_of_the_0.8095.note: 0.81 of the 0.81 general-index ceiling is FULL-HEADROOM imputation for the 5 domains + 2 benchmarks that were never censused. The MEASURED slice (D3) contributes 0. This is the whole point: a 1-domain census cannot kill a 6-domain index.

g8 Mathlib coverage 0/1000 (registry/verdicts/g8.json) — cited context, not re-run here.

delta_max simUCB95 = 0.8095 is a MEASURED UPPER BOUND on the general-index gain given THIS partial census and THIS provisional pin (the bound is computed, not simulated; the rig is deterministic and stdlib-only).
The D3-domain-own-axis ceiling = 0.0000: on the censused D3 slice, perfect covered-item correction yields exactly zero index gain because 0 items are covered.
STIPULATED: Every weight, chance, ceiling, and delta_k in index-pin.PROVISIONAL.json (frozen=false).
The D4-hypothetical census coverage and covered-baseline counts (placeholder).
label_fn_ub values (0.0 licensed only for the deterministic mapper classifier; 0.02 is a placeholder for RUN-2).
EXTRAPOLATION_the_coordinators_to_draw: Whether the general-index-mover (W1) claim is DEAD. This rig CANNOT establish that from existing data: a partial census can only make a whole-index kill HARDER, never easier (POWER.md §3.1). The 0.8095 bound is dominated by full-headroom imputation, so it does NOT license a general-index kill. The maintainer/coordinator must NOT read '0/1550' as a general-index kill.

Whether a DOMAIN-SCOPED (D3 / biomedical-define) kill fires. The MEASURED D3 own-axis ceiling of 0.0000 < delta_k^D3 = 0.05 WOULD fire a domain-scoped kill — but only on a verdict-eligible run (frozen pin + census pin block + registered Form-A eligibility). It is currently CONTINGENT (mapper-parse lane) and ILLUSTRATIVE-ONLY.

does_it_kill_the_general_index_mover_cla.why: The existing censuses cover exactly ONE domain (D3) of a six-domain provisional index. The completeness rule (no silent zeros) imputes full headroom to the 5 uncensused domains + the 2 uncensused D4 benchmarks, so delta_max simUCB95 = 0.8095 >> delta_k = 0.03. A general-index kill requires simUCB95(delta_max) < delta_k, which is arithmetically unreachable while any material index weight is uncensused.
does_it_kill_the_general_index_mover_cla.correction_to_steering_premise: The steering note (round1-steering.md item 3) states 'the general-index W1 ambition is probably already dead' and expects this run to kill it with zero GPU. Run honestly, the rig does NOT deliver that kill from existing data — it delivers (a) a MEASURED zero-coverage D3 slice that would support a DOMAIN-SCOPED kill once eligibility lands, and (b) the arithmetic proof that no whole-index kill is possible from a one-domain census. This is a material steering correction: the free kill that exists is domain-scoped, not general-index.

real_data_still_needed_for_a_verdict_eli: A FROZEN index pin (P3-D-INDEX / KOT-AI-INDEX/2): frozen=true, with registered weights, chance/ceiling/metric_type per benchmark for EVERY benchmark of EVERY weighted domain (D1,D2,D3,D4,D5,D6). The provisional pin has frozen=false and 4 empty weighted domains.

A coverage census over the WHOLE frozen R-1 suite (not just the 7 D3 define-lane benchmarks): b-cov-smol + G-CODE-0 + the P3-X-SIEVE-CENSUS lemma-touch upper sieve (mechanical, genuinely cheap, gives UNCONDITIONAL kills) and/or the P3-X-ORACLE-CENSUS (human-annotated, budgeted, NOT ~$0).
The full census pin block (model_checkpoint, harness_hash, prompt_policy, seeds, store_hash, kernel_hash, world_layer_hash, mapper_hash, grammar_version, benchmark_revisions).
A registered Form-A family-eligibility (noninterference / covered-support) reference for each store-dependent family (P3-D-ELIG).
Integer covered-subset baseline counts (correct_cov / n_cov) from P3-E-CAL / P3-X-COVBASE — only needed where coverage > 0; irrelevant while kappa = 0 (a_cov imputed 0 correctly).
For a DOMAIN-SCOPED D3 kill specifically: only the frozen pin + census pin block + eligibility ref are missing; the coverage measurement (0/1550) already exists. That is the cheap, near-term win.
